User Story: As a data engineer, I want to ingest live earthquake feeds so that seismic events are available for prediction.
As a data engineer, I want to integrate flood and rainfall proxy feeds so that hydro-risk is updated continuously.
As a data engineer, I want to store normalized multi-hazard records in a unified database so that downstream modules read consistent features.
As an ML engineer, I want a random-forest-based risk classifier so that hazard levels can be predicted from environmental features.
As an ML engineer, I want rule-based fallback logic so that predictions remain available when model confidence is low.
As a researcher, I want feature-importance extraction so that model decisions can be interpreted in reports.
As a disaster analyst, I want 0–7 day risk trend modeling so that early warning behavior can be monitored over time.
As a disaster analyst, I want hazard-wise risk aggregation by region so that local authorities can compare hotspots.
As a system architect, I want confidence-aware trend smoothing so that noisy event spikes do not trigger unstable warnings.
As an emergency officer, I want advisory/watch/warning threshold bands so that alerts map directly to action levels.
As an emergency officer, I want automatic alert generation from predicted risk so that response teams are notified without delay.
As a citizen, I want hazard-specific mitigation guidance so that I can take preventive action immediately.
As a local planner, I want mitigation recommendations linked to risk level so that resource allocation is prioritized effectively.
As a command-center operator, I want a live dashboard for earthquakes, floods, and landslides so that situational awareness is centralized.
As a researcher, I want publication-ready analytics charts so that project outcomes can be reported in conference review documents.
